business.headline: Trump sons-backed Aureus to merge with drone firm Powerus targeting Pentagon salesDrones have become a major procurement priority for the Pentagon and are widely used in Ukraine, where dense air defense systems near the front lines limit the deployment of conventional aircraft.

Stock markets sink worldwide as oil prices briefly spike close to $120 per barrelThe S&P 500 fell 1.3 per cent, coming off its worst week since October. The Dow Jones Industrial Average was down 721 points, or 1.5 per cent, as of 9:35 am Eastern time, and the Nasdaq composite was 1.2 per cent lower. That followed even worse losses in European and Asian stock markets.

G7 nations hold off on emergency oil stock release as Gulf crisis lifts pricesThe bloc of the world's seven most powerful economies said it is prepared to act to stabilise oil markets if needed but believes the situation has not yet reached a stage requiring coordinated measures such as a joint release of strategic petroleum stocks.

Keep your PPF, SSY, NPS active: Complete this before March 31 deadlineThe March 31 deadline is almost here! Ensure your PPF, SSY, and NPS contributions are up-to-date to avoid penalties and keep your accounts running smoothly.

FM Nirmala Sitharaman explainsReplying in writing in Parliament, Nirmala Sitharaman said domestic price levels are currently near the lower end of the Reserve Bank of India's (RBI) tolerance band, which should help limit the impact of higher crude prices on inflation.

Is the world ready for the 'unthinkable' amid West Asia conflict? IMF chief asksGeorgieva said shipping activity through the Strait of Hormuz has dropped by about 90%. The narrow waterway is one of the most important energy routes in the world.

How 'lucky number 7' helped unravel an alleged cement cartelThe issue surfaced during a 2018 tender by ONGC for a cement order. When the bids were opened, the company noticed that all competing firms had quoted exactly Rs 7,000 per tonne.

Will your pocket feel the pinch?While the government has said crude oil imports are continuing in "full flow" through alternative routes and that energy supplies remain secure for now, analysts warn that a prolonged spike in crude prices could still affect consumers if the rally continues.

The company added that it has adequate legal and factual grounds to challenge the demand and will take the necessary steps to protect its interests.

SBI share price falls 6%: Should you sell or hold the PSU stock?The share price fall wiped out nearly Rs 62,352 crore in market capitalisation. With this drop, the bank's valuation slipped below the Rs 10 lakh crore mark to around Rs 9.93 lakh crore.

Rupee falls to record low amid crude oil shock: What it means for IndiaThe rupee dropped 0.6% to 92.3350 against the US dollar, breaking its previous record low of 92.3025 that was touched last week. Rupee has remained under pressure since the United States and Israel launched military action against Iran last week.

Stock market crash: IndiGo, SBI, Maruti among worst hit amid ongoing sell-offAmong the worst-hit stocks on the index were InterGlobe Aviation (IndiGo) and State Bank of India (SBI), reflecting the broad-based nature of the market decline.

Kareena, Ananya Panday and Kalyani Priyadarshan on hypermasculine trend in films Actors Kareena Kapoor Khan, Kalyani Priyadarshan and Ananya Panday shared their views on the growing trend of hypermasculine male characters in Indian cinema.

What Vikram Bhatt posted about Vijay-Trisha amid wedding outing, divorce case Filmmaker Vikram Bhatt defended Vijay and Trisha after the two were spotted together at a wedding amid the actor-politician's divorce battle. Kamal Haasan reportedly highest-paid actor in Kalki 2898 AD. Can you guess his fee? Vishal Dadlani calls Arijit Singh a genius, says follow your heart Vishal Dadlani voiced support for Arijit Singh after the singer announced he is stepping away from playback singing. Vishal called Arijit a musical genius and encouraged him to follow what makes him happy.

Shakira adds second Mumbai show after massive fan response. Check dates, venue Global music star Shakira will perform three times in India this April, adding a second Mumbai concert due to overwhelming interest and making her first major return to the country in nearly 19 years.

general news.headline: Trump reviewing options to bring soaring oil prices under control: ReportWith oil prices soaring above $100 a barrel due to the Iran conflict, President Trump is set to consider emergency measures to stabilise fuel costs and energy markets. The White House is actively exploring options ahead of the US midterm elections to mitigate economic impacts on businesses and consumers.

What President's U-turn, Mojtaba's appointment revealAlthough Iran's leadership remains united in continuing the fight against the US and Israel, signs of division are emerging in Tehran. The backlash over President Masoud Pezeshkian's apology for attacks on neighbouring states, and the appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as the supreme leader despite resistance, suggest growing discord.

Live: Trump calls choice of Iran's new Supreme Leader 'a big mistake'US-Israel Attacks Iran Live Updates: As the Middle East war entered its 10th day, Iran launched fresh attacks on Israel and several Gulf countries on Monday, hours after state television reported that Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the country's late supreme leader, had been named his successor. The escalation rattled global markets, sending oil prices sharply higher and sparking fears that rising energy costs could fuel inflation and curb consumer spending in the United States. Meanwhile, Saudi Arabia warned Tehran it would be the "biggest loser" if attacks on Arab states continued after a drone strike targeted its Shaybah oil field. Follow indiatoday.in for all latest updates on the Middle East crisis.

Is your job in the red zone? The Anthropic AI chart shows which careers are changingThe debate around AI and jobs often swings between hype and alarm, but a new visual based on research by Anthropic brings a rare dose of clarity to the conversation. By comparing AI's theoretical capabilities with how workers are using it today, it sketches an early map of how the modern workplace is shifting. The question now is,  where do you stand on that map?

sports.headline: Stranded West Indies, South Africa to leave India on March 10 South Africa and West Indies, who were unable to return home due to a lack of flight availability, are finally set to depart for home on March 10. The teams had been stranded in India following widespread airspace closures triggered by the Israel–Iran conflict.

Samson scored three consecutive fifties after returning to the playing XI and helping India win the title.
